title: salix acutata
intro: Salix acutata, also known as the mountain willow or sharp-leaved willow, is a shrub or small tree species within the willow family (Salicaceae) that is native to New Zealand and parts of Australia. The medicinal properties of Salix acutata include its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic, making it useful for treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Salix acutata tea or infusions include lowering blood pressure, reducing fever, and providing relief from rheumatism and arthritis due to its anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, Salix acutata is often combined with other herbs to create topical creams and ointments, as well as teas and infusions for internal use.
